# Predictive History

> A source-disciplined public study companion for Professor Jiang's Predictive History corpus.
> Start with one source item at a time. Use `chapter-manifest.yaml` to route to the canonical source transcript, commentary, and civ-ph placement card, and use the docs below for trust, fidelity, and export rules.

## Start Here

- [README.md](README.md): human-facing overview and contribution path.
- [FOR-PROFESSOR-JIANG.md](FOR-PROFESSOR-JIANG.md): Jiang-facing posture page for unofficial status, correction paths, and course-improvement signals.
- [chapter-manifest.yaml](chapter-manifest.yaml): machine-readable chapter routing table.
- [docs/repo-map.md](docs/repo-map.md): folder map and surface guide.

## Surface Vocabulary

- Source items are the underlying Jiang videos, essays, interviews, or other sources.
- Source transcripts are source-text or transcript records when available.
- Commentaries are the main study surfaces for thesis, claims, concepts, predictions, counter-readings, routes, limits, and correction paths.
- civ-ph placement cards are compact civilizational orientation and re-entry pages.
- Part One and Part Two refer only to the corpus architecture: Civilization and Apocalypse.

## Canonical Rules

- [docs/source-status.md](docs/source-status.md): source, transcript, annotation, review, and fidelity vocabulary.
- [docs/annotation-block.md](docs/annotation-block.md): public item template.
- [docs/audience-profile.md](docs/audience-profile.md): internal audience segments, trust tensions, and product posture for corpus and `civ-ph` work.
- [docs/predictive-history-after-tolstoy.md](docs/predictive-history-after-tolstoy.md): front-door causation essay; use it when a task asks what Predictive History is responding to in Tolstoy or great-man history.
- [docs/predictive-history-museum.md](docs/predictive-history-museum.md): canonical Predictive History Museum storage and bidder contract; URL-only artifacts are insufficient.
- [docs/ph-choreography.md](docs/ph-choreography.md): public-surface choreography for `ph-civ`, `ph-apo`, and `ph-mus`; use it to route between historical formation, pressure application, and museum encounter.
- [docs/ph-civ-publication-gate.md](docs/ph-civ-publication-gate.md): export gate; `ph-civ` population remains paused until route-level status permits export.
- [docs/ph-civ-pre-publication-release.md](docs/ph-civ-pre-publication-release.md): pre-publication scaffold for `pilot-001`, public front-door requirements, and empty states.
- [docs/tolstoy-lens.md](docs/tolstoy-lens.md): bounded commentary standard for Jiang Lens / Tolstoy Lens choreography and actor-pressure causation sections.
- [docs/export-from-strategy-codex.md](docs/export-from-strategy-codex.md): one-way boundary from Strategy-Codex and transcript export rules.
- [docs/corrections-policy.md](docs/corrections-policy.md): how to report and review corrections.
- [FOR-PROFESSOR-JIANG.md](FOR-PROFESSOR-JIANG.md): public posture for Professor Jiang or course-team review; treat it as the canonical statement that the repo is unofficial, corrigible, and open to correction or supersession.
- [docs/internal-audit-substrate.md](docs/internal-audit-substrate.md): maintainer audit workflow and tracked health reports.
- [docs/recursive-improvement-loops.md](docs/recursive-improvement-loops.md): internal quality loops for validation, audit, review queues, deepening, and revalidation.
- [docs/youtube-transcript-workflow.md](docs/youtube-transcript-workflow.md): PH YouTube transcript layers, quote-grade boundaries, and skill routing.

## Canonical Content

- [book/parts/](book/parts/): semester guides for Civilization and Apocalypse.
- [book/parts/civilization-to-apocalypse.md](book/parts/civilization-to-apocalypse.md): transition essay from Civilization's law discovery to Apocalypse's pressure application.
- [book/volume-i/](book/volume-i/): Geo-Strategy source transcripts and commentaries.
- [book/volume-ii/](book/volume-ii/): Civilization chapter scaffold and first sixty-chapter spine.
- [book/volume-iii/](book/volume-iii/): Game Theory source transcripts and commentaries.
- [book/volume-v/](book/volume-v/): Great Books chapter scaffold and first ten-chapter spine.
- [book/volume-vi/](book/volume-vi/): Secret History source transcripts and commentaries.
- [book/volume-ii/civ-01/](book/volume-ii/civ-01/): calibrated Civilization pilot source transcript and commentary.
- [corpus/civ-ph/](corpus/civ-ph/): derived civ-ph placement cards and return paths for calibrated and in-review units.
- [corpus/cross-volume/](corpus/cross-volume/): public guided routes across volumes, beginning with Homer to Dante and the broader Homer to Tolstoy literary arc.
- [corpus/geo-strategy/](corpus/geo-strategy/): Geo-Strategy source pointers and public indices.
- [corpus/game-theory/](corpus/game-theory/): Game Theory source pointers and public indices.
- [corpus/civilization/](corpus/civilization/): Civilization source pointers for `civ-01` through `civ-60`.
- [corpus/great-books/](corpus/great-books/): Great Books source pointers for `gb-01` through `gb-10`.
- [corpus/secret-history/](corpus/secret-history/): Secret History source pointers for `sh-01` through `sh-28`.
- [corpus/world-war/](corpus/world-war/): pressure corridors for the applied second semester.
- [corpus/media-packs/](corpus/media-packs/): Predictive History Museum exhibits; use before transcript or commentary when the task asks which artifacts students should encounter first. Target one exhibit per chapter across Predictive History: Civilization and Predictive History: Apocalypse. Artifacts may be visual, textual, sonic, spatial, institutional, or symbolic.
- [registries/sources.md](registries/sources.md): current source registry entries.
- [registries/cross-volume-links.yaml](registries/cross-volume-links.yaml): typed edges for cross-volume corridors.
- [registries/causation-lenses.yaml](registries/causation-lenses.yaml): actor-pressure pairs for the Tolstoy question.
- [registries/ph-civ-export-manifest.yaml](registries/ph-civ-export-manifest.yaml): named pre-publication batch manifest; batch membership does not approve export.
- [registries/ph-claim-boundaries.yaml](registries/ph-claim-boundaries.yaml): claim boundary taxonomy for public-surface readiness.
- [docs/chapter-index.md](docs/chapter-index.md): human-readable chapter catalog.
- [docs/series-roadmap.md](docs/series-roadmap.md): series-level planning and next-batch order.
- [reports/](reports/): internal tracked audit snapshots for spine health, civ-ph health, review queue, and next actions.
- [reports/improvement-loops.md](reports/improvement-loops.md): internal summary of recursive quality-loop health.
- [reports/review-sprint.md](reports/review-sprint.md): weekly maintainer sprint selected from the high-risk review queue.

## Load Contract

- Default to one chapter at a time.
- Use `civ_ph_path` from `chapter-manifest.yaml` when the task asks for placement, orientation, or re-entry framing; check `review_status` before treating a civ-ph entry as calibrated.
- Treat Part One, Civilization, as the law-discovery semester; treat Part Two, Apocalypse, as the law-application semester.
- Use `book/parts/civilization-to-apocalypse.md` when a task asks how the two parts connect.
- Use `corpus/cross-volume/` and `registries/cross-volume-links.yaml` when the task asks for cross-volume inheritance, comparison, or a guided route across series.
- Use `docs/tolstoy-lens.md`, `corpus/cross-volume/tolstoy-question.md`, and `registries/causation-lenses.yaml` when the task asks whether named actors make history or reveal deeper pressure systems. Only add a Tolstoy Lens section when the source ID appears in the causation lens registry.
- Treat civ-ph work as `map -> orient -> constrain -> validate -> route`.
- Treat media-pack work as Predictive History Museum work: humans curate artifacts and provide taste, cultural judgment, proportion, emotional calibration, and final responsibility; agents structure payloads, write museum labels, validate links, and preserve link-first rights-safe status.
- Predictive History Museum artifacts must be stored in both a local museum vault and shared document cloud workspace; GitHub stores manifests and generated pages, not large binary artifacts.
- Treat `ph-civ` as not populated until a route passes the publication gate. If a route is paused, use the empty-state language from `docs/ph-civ-pre-publication-release.md`.
- Use the repo-local skill suite by task: `ph-transcript`, `ph-chapter`, `civ-ph`, `ph-cross-volume`, and `ph-audit`.
- Treat `ph-youtube-transcript` as a compatibility alias for `ph-transcript`.
- Expand to adjacent chapters only for comparison tasks.
- Treat chronology as the primary navigation axis and topic paths as secondary.
